name: @anistark/iconify
description: Turn a logo into a full favicon set from your terminal — pi extension + CLI
readme:
# @anistark/iconify

[![npm version](https://img.shields.io/npm/v/@anistark/iconify.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@anistark/iconify)
[![npm downloads](https://img.shields.io/npm/dm/@anistark/iconify.svg)](https://www.npmjs.com/package/@anistark/iconify)
[![license](https://img.shields.io/npm/l/@anistark/iconify.svg)](https://opensource.org/licenses/MIT)
[![website](https://img.shields.io/badge/website-iconify.dev-1e90ff)](https://iconify.dev)
[![pi extension](https://img.shields.io/badge/pi-extension-7c3aed)](https://pi.dev)

Turn a logo image into a favicon set from your terminal — ships a CLI, a [pi](https://pi.dev) extension, and is the engine behind the iconify Claude Code plugin. The web version lives at [iconify.dev](https://iconify.dev).

## Install

```bash
# pi extension
pi install npm:@anistark/iconify

# global CLI
npm i -g @anistark/iconify

# or one-shot via npx
npx @anistark/iconify logo.png
```

## Usage

```
iconify <image-path> [size] [OUTPUT_PATH=path]
```

- `<image-path>` — input image (PNG/JPEG/SVG/WebP/GIF/BMP/TIFF/AVIF; resolved relative to cwd).
- `[size]` — optional integer. If given, writes a single `icon-NxN.png` at that size. Omit to write the full set: `favicon-16x16.ico` + `icon-{16,48,128}x*.png`.
- `OUTPUT_PATH=path` — output directory. Defaults to the current working directory.

### Examples

```bash
iconify logo.png                              # full set in cwd
iconify logo.svg 64                           # just icon-64x64.png in cwd
iconify logo.png OUTPUT_PATH=./public         # full set in ./public
iconify logo.png 256 OUTPUT_PATH=./out        # single 256x256 PNG in ./out
```

### Inside pi

```
/iconify logo.png
/iconify logo.png 64
/iconify logo.png OUTPUT_PATH=./out
```

The `iconify` LLM tool is also registered, so the agent can generate icons mid-conversation.

## License

MIT
